title: Polyplethia formosana
intro: Polyplethia formosana, also known as Formosa angelica or Taiwanese angelica, is a perennial plant species in the carrot family (Apiaceae) native to Taiwan. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Polyplethia formosana include polyacetylenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from Polyplethia formosana include tea infusions, decoctions, and dried extracts, which are commonly used in Taiwanese folk medicine to alleviate various health conditions.
